summary: This page explains that a system’s external input-output description can fail to capture its full internal structure, as illustrated by an example where an internal capacitor is invisible from the external terminals. It discusses how uncontrollable and unobservable subsystems lead external measurements to characterize only the controllable and observable portion of a system, and notes that such systems are undesirable in practice.

figures.caption: Fig. 1.34 A system that cannot be described by external measurements.
Fig. 1.35 Structures of uncontrollable and unobservable systems.
